history.",[2420],{"id":2421,"name":2422,"slug":1650},"6ce988a6-114b-4569-91bb-a579fa746f3c","Movie",{"byline":1289,"firstName":2424,"id":2425,"lastName":2426,"username":2427},"Liam","eca1288f-e3e3-4535-86e4-d4b1e23ef848","Mathews","liammathewsmc",{"id":2429,"filename":2430,"dateCreated":2431,"alt":1289,"credits":2434,"path":2435,"cropGravity":1289,"crop":1289,"caption":2436,"typeName":1289,"imageUrl":1289,"width":2437,"height":2438,"sType":1289,"bucketType":1289,"bucketPath":1289,"mediaType":1289},"c6d5fc1b-bfd6-4d23-beb8-cabf1eadf09b","mainmusicbiopics.jpg",{"date":2432,"timezone":2433},"2026-04-22 18:11:26.000000","UTC","\"Amadeus\" (Orion Pictures)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2026/04/22/c6d5fc1b-bfd6-4d23-beb8-cabf1eadf09b/mainmusicbiopics.jpg","\u003Cp>Biographical films about musicians are  popular  with  audiences and  awards voters. But it's an uneven genre for  critics. Playing a famous   singer is a tried-and-true way to win an Oscar (ask Rami Malek, Reese   Witherspoon, Jamie Foxx, etc.), but the movies themselves are often  dismissed  by reviewers as cliched. The story arc of a music biopic is  so formulaic that it  was parodied in \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/movie/walk-hard-the-dewey-cox-story/\">Walk Hard: The Dewey Cox Story\u003C/a> nearly 20 years  ago.&nbsp;\u003C/p>\u003Cp>But some music biopics are a note above the rest. this  listˇŞhere are the 20 best-reviewed music  biopics, ranked by Metascore  (with higher numbers on our 0-100 scale equating  to better overall  reviews from leading professional by Metascore.",[2469],{"id":2421,"name":2422,"slug":1650},{"byline":1289,"firstName":2471,"id":2472,"lastName":2473,"username":2474},"Nick","7974d639-0d7a-4f9d-bc19-92d8e0ac3199","Hyman","nickhyman",{"id":2476,"filename":2477,"dateCreated":2478,"alt":1289,"credits":2480,"path":2481,"cropGravity":1289,"crop":1289,"caption":2482,"typeName":1289,"imageUrl":1289,"width":2437,"height":2438,"sType":1289,"bucketType":1289,"bucketPath":1289,"mediaType":1289},"b487099c-39e0-4558-9ae5-ee1effedf71b","mainbenwheatleymovies.jpg",{"date":2479,"timezone":2433},"2026-04-14 21:50:41.000000","\"Normal\" (Magnolia Pictures)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2026/04/14/b487099c-39e0-4558-9ae5-ee1effedf71b/mainbenwheatleymovies.jpg","\u003Cp>For nearly two decades, British filmmaker Ben Wheatley has  carved  out a unique space as one of modern cinema's most audacious,   unpredictable, and polarizing directors. His work defiantly resists easy   categorization, swerving from gritty crime thrillers and chilling folk  horror  to pitch-black comedy, dystopian satire, and studio  blockbusters. This eclectic  filmography is united by a distinctive  style often marked by visceral  violence, psychedelic imagery, and a  wickedly dark sense of humor. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Wheatley's  career trajectory has seen him evolve from a scrappy,  micro-budget indie  darling into a director capable of helming  large-scale studio productions. This  journey has produced a fascinating  range of critical reactions, from universal  acclaim to widespread  bewilderment. With Wheatley's Bob Odenkirk-starring small town  crime  thriller \u003Ca href=\"file:///C:/Users/JasonDietz/Dropbox/CBS%20Metacritic/Freelancer%20Assignments/xx\">Normal\u003C/a>  hitting theaters this week, let's rank all* of his feature  films by  their Metascores (utilizing a 0¨C100 scale to reflect the  consensus of  top professional critics). The ranking serves as a map through the  career of a truly  singular and uncompromising artist, showcasing the  highs, lows, and brilliant,  chaotic spaces in between. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>*Omitted, for now, is his recent  festival curio \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/movie/bulk/\">Bulk\u003C/a>, which has yet to open commercially.\u003C/p>",{"id":2484,"type":2414,"title":1412,"slug":2485,"date":2486,"url":1289,"isVideo":1289,"description":2487,"tags":2488,"author":2490,"image":2495},"094ef395-15df-4230-ac65-2f2f9a0df79e","april-2026-movie-preview-super-mario-galaxy-michael","2026-03-30 21:24:00","Our editors select the most noteworthy films debuting in April 2026, led by The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, Michael, The Drama, and more.",[2489],{"id":2421,"name":2422,"slug":1650},{"byline":1289,"firstName":2491,"id":2492,"lastName":2493,"username":2494},"Keith","c72d7a8f-c754-4842-8f22-0575700c03ab","Kimbell","kkimbell-mc",{"id":2496,"filename":2497,"dateCreated":2498,"alt":1289,"credits":2500,"path":2501,"cropGravity":1289,"crop":1289,"caption":2502,"typeName":1289,"imageUrl":1289,"width":2437,"height":2438,"sType":1289,"bucketType":1289,"bucketPath":1289,"mediaType":1289},"bbd905d9-0d36-4b35-830f-45d8d33d57d8","moviepreview-2026-04-supermariogalaxy.jpg",{"date":2499,"timezone":2433},"2026-03-30 21:25:36.000000","\"The Super Mario Galaxy Movie\" (Universal/Illumination/Nintendo)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2026/03/30/bbd905d9-0d36-4b35-830f-45d8d33d57d8/moviepreview-2026-04-supermariogalaxy.jpg","\u003Cp>The month ahead will bring a \u003Cem>Super Mario Galaxy\u003C/em> movie, Antoine Fuqua's Michael Jackson biopic, Zendaya/Robert Pattinson team-up \u003Cem>The Drama\u003C/em>, the Charli XCX-starring \u003Cem>Erupcja\u003C/em>,  and new films by Steven Soderbergh, Ben Wheatley, and more. As Riley and her emotions struggle to adjust to a new life in San Francisco, turmoil ensues in Headquarters. Kindred spirits, Varda and JR share a lifelong passion for images and how they are created, displayed and shared. Together they travel around the villages of France in JRˇŻs photo truck meeting locals, learning their stories and producing epic-size portraits of them. The photos are prominently displayed on houses, barns, storefronts and trains revealing the humanity in their subjects, and themselves. Faces Places documents these heart-warming encounters as well as the unlikely, tender friendship they formed along the way. Loved it!","2025-04-13","strangebrew123",1744543827623,{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3374,"quote":3375,"score":2378,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3376,"author":3377,"version":3378,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3379},"48467d16-b2d2-42d6-aa17-ade5195227c3","Wow. Probably one of, if not, the best movies of all time. The acting, cinematography, and music come together to show the fantastic epic.","2025-03-01","1007Conradical",1740790941027,{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3381,"quote":3382,"score":859,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3383,"author":3384,"version":3385,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3386},"eeb75e47-c841-4b04-a790-cd009aff0b88","The sandy front of World War I, set amongst the dunes and political turmoil of Syria, Egypt and Jordan, as seen by a **** British army officer with maybe a little bit of a messiah complex. A true epic, from the breath-stealing long shots of an unimaginably vast desert to the explosive, well-outfitted battle scenes, it lives up to the billing and then some. I was mesmerized by the photography in particular, which constantly discovers new ways to portray what could have been a rather redundant, flat, boring landscape. Truly beautiful, almost ruminative at times, with an appreciation for the artisanship of film.\n\nAs for the plot, well... it's a bit slow. Particularly so in the nearly four-hour director's cut, which feels completely unnecessary. I've never seen the theatrical release, which is noted for being incomprehensible in places due to the awkward cuts, but it seems suspicious that half an hour or more couldn't have been lopped off somewhere to improve the pace. Lawrence of Arabia is an exhausting watch, and not just because of the draining personal transformation we see in the title character. There's good material, of course, plenty of it. The hopeless inner squabble of an Arab population that's desperately close to the unified independence they seek. The protagonist's progression from philosophical humanist to enraged berzerker to **** shell. We get plenty to think about, long after the credits have finally, mercifully rolled, but it's a marathon to reach that point.\n\nA Best Picture winner that's technically marvelous, thematically challenging and open-minded, but the ending left me feeling cold and there's really no excuse for it to run for as long as it does.","2024-09-21","drqshadow",1726922368939,{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3388,"quote":3389,"score":859,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3390,"author":3391,"version":3392,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3393},"fca8b4aa-a695-4de8-8026-a632ab40fa3f","It's long, it's dated, it's an old style Hollywood epic. One of the best of the last if you are into that sort of thing. All 8 critics here have given it 100 points, but what do they know?\nHas some beautiful shots and some very long ones. I watched on TV, albeit an enormous screen so quality of image and sound were not diminished, on the downside I paused it few times for food and phone breaks.","2019-12-14","311433",1576325668000,{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3395,"quote":3396,"score":26,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3397,"author":3398,"version":3399,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3400},"2e7ed67e-c043-41ca-9244-0564451f9adb","I found out one very interesting fact about this film users review is \"CONSISTENCY\" ... all the negative critics users written are very very similar like:\r\n- film too long (3.50h)\r\n- film too boring\r\n- film has poor story\r\n\r\nThe users which gave negative critics also pointed out some positive thing found in the film similar like:\r\n- film has great photography (desert scenes/animal scenes camels, horses...)\r\n- film has some great acting performances (but boring story and very long scenes spoiled my interest)\r\n\r\nMY CONCLUSION:\r\nLike the most of the negative user critics I had very similar experience. I was actually very disappointed because I've read so many good critics from users before I watched this film all saying it is EPIC film but as I watched it -> partially/fully overrated... It deserves only three stars and no more. I shoul next time read aso the negative critics before reading positive to see the difference. But likely the critics which are many places consistent and repetitive are likely \"genuine critics\" or real personal experience. Im not sure if all this positive critics found on board are \"truly genuine critics\" because film making is big money today much bigger then before we are talking of billions of $$$...so what to think... I learn every time something new... one has to seems read also the negative comments/ratings and if there is consistency there is great chance of genuine viewers experience it means I may also not like this film.... chances are 50-70%....","2016-10-07","virtualPresence",1475826755000,{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3402,"quote":3403,"score":26,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3404,"author":3405,"version":3406,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3407},"6e16542b-14e8-4667-b2ac-0f3cb983ceac","I watched this movie with no doubt that it would be as good as it was made out to be. It was in the top 10 of the AFI top 100 movies of all time, and it had received many 4 star reviews. About an hour into the film, I fell asleep. Now, fans of the film would say, \"Oh, you can't handle the impact of a long epic film. You have a short attention span.\" Well, with me, that is not the case. But, on the subject of length, this film is almost four hours long. For the content displayed in this film, the running time was way too long. If Titanic can cram all the hefty things it needed in a matter of 3 hours, then this is no excuse. Nothing was happening. I mean, literally nothing. The screenplay needs a real doctor, because it has so much unneeded dialogue. It just procrastinated its way through, I think because it really had nothing more interesting. Director David lean is probably one of the best directors of all time, but in this film, you can tell that his and the producer's mind frame was \"I wonder how many academy awards I can get through this film?\" It wasn't made naturally, it was gargantuan for this point. I about turned off the movie when I saw a thirty second look at the desert landscape for about the millionth time. Yes, it does take place in the desert, but this movie was called \"Lawrence of Arabia,\" not \"The Arabian Desert.\" Which brings me to my next point: Peter O' Toole. I have seen him in many marvelous performances. I'm surprised he hasn't received an Oscar yet. But, his interpretation of Lawrence was so vague. You only saw the exterior, you never saw the interior. He gets a rush out of killing, yet we don't know why. i partially blame it on the script writers, but I also partially blame it on him. When you are an actor, you have to convey to the audience who you are, no matter how good or bad the script is. he is playing the character that embodies the whole movie. It is solely about him, and he doesn't sell that. Now, I'm not going to bash everything just because I don't like the film, but i will compliment Alec Guinness for his outstanding but short performance as Prince Feisal, Maurice Jarre for the superb score, and the editing.  Sorry to burst the bubble of the \"Lawrence of Arabia\" fans, but I really think this film is overrated, and that it is not as good as it is made out to be. That is my opinion on \"Lawrence of Arabia.\"","2011-06-23","Batman30512",1308836747000,{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},[3409,3411,3413,3415,3422,3429,3436],{"id":3360,"quote":3361,"score":2378,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3362,"author":3363,"version":3364,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3410},{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3367,"quote":3368,"score":2378,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3369,"author":3370,"version":3371,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3412},{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3374,"quote":3375,"score":2378,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3376,"author":3377,"version":3378,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3414},{"id":1649,"type":3240,"title":1651,"url":3241},{"id":3416,"quote":3417,"score":2378,"thumbsUp":1289,"thumbsDown":1289,"date":3418,"author":3419,"version":3420,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3421},"eb979410-b6f6-492e-9156-de08bc7fcf58","Peter O' Toole is a legend and a rare diamond amongst the rough. Lawrence as a hero while depicting the Ottoman Turks as cruel oppressors. It ignores the historical complexity of the conflict and portrays the Turks as villains, fueling anti-Turkish sentiment. You should not expect an adventure story as many sites list it, instead you will find the rest are true; a Biography War Drama. A perfect comparison to this story is the movie: 'The Ten Commandments'. An extremely long look at a piece of history. This film fails as a solely entertaining experience. If you do not want to know this piece of (biased)history then don't watch this movie. I watched this because it was voted top Adventure movie in many places, but I would not call this an adventure, more a biography as stated before. There is no structure to make this an interesting and exciting story, just a historical retelling with only its events as pacing, making it slow and unsuspenseful.\nIt certainly didn't need the extra hour and a half it took, but lacking any structure they decided to show everything they had and cut nothing.\n\nOverall I liked the movie despite the score, but it certainly is no adventure movie or anything worth 3 and a half hours unless you're into movies like the ten commandments.
